scene.title: Simple steel portal frame
scene.description: Two-column steel frame with one horizontal beam — ~4 m wide × 3 m tall

objects.label: Left column
objects.note: 200 mm square steel post, 3 m tall

objects.label: Right column
objects.note: 200 mm square steel post, 3 m tall

objects.label: Top beam
objects.note: Horizontal beam spanning ~4 m, 250 mm deep, bearing on both columns

messages.content:
👋 I'm **Grok** — your co-builder.

We design **in 3D together**. Tell me anything you can imagine:
- *“Build a steel pedestrian bridge”*
- *“Make a 6-DOF robot arm”*
- *“Design a tiny Mars habitat”*
- *“A wooden cabin with a loft”*

I'll place real geometry you can orbit, and **we can change it anytime**. I remember everything we build and talk about.

What should we create first?
Build a simple two-column steel frame with one horizontal beam. Keep it simple, about 4m wide and 3m tall.

Built a **simple two-column steel portal frame** — clean and readable for a first structural build.

### What you have
| Member | Size | Position |
|--------|------|----------|
| **2 columns** | 200 × 200 mm square HSS-style | Centers at **x = ±2.0 m** (4 m c/c), height **3.0 m** |
| **1 beam** | **4.2 m** long × 250 mm deep × 200 mm wide | Sits on the column tops |

- **Overall:** ~4 m wide × 3 m tall (to underside of beam; top of beam ≈ **3.25 m**)
- **Material look:** cool steel gray with higher metalness so it reads as steel in the viewport
- Columns stand on the ground plane; beam rests on both posts like a simple portal / goal-post frame

**Assumption (not PE-stamped):** this is schematic geometry for learning proportions — not sized for real loads, base plates, or connections.

### Try next
- Add **base plates** and **anchor bolts**
- Make it a **moment frame** (haunches / end plates)
- Switch to **wide-flange** proportions or add a second bay
- Change height/span (“taller”, “6 m wide”, etc.)

Orbit the viewport and tell me what to change.
